item.title: Yann LeCun’s AMI Labs raises $1.03 billion to build world models
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: AMI Labs, the new venture cofounded by Turing Prize winner Yann LeCun after he left Meta, has raised $1.03 billion at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation.

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber steps down
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Graber explained that, as a more mature company, Bluesky needs a "seasoned operator focused on scaling and execution." Graber said she feels better suited to building Bluesky's technology itself.

item.title: Periwinkle is making self-hosted social media on Bluesky’s AT Protocol even easier
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Periwinkle offers managed hosting for AT Protocol users who want social media accounts on their own domains, with backups, storage, and migration tools.

item.title: Palmer Luckey’s retro gaming startup ModRetro reportedly seeks funding at $1B valuation
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The company launched its first product, a Game Boy-style handheld device called the Chromatic, in 2024.

item.title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The fund currently offers retail investors exposure to eight startups, including Mercor, Ramp, and Stripe, with plans to expand its portfolio.

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon, the SaaSpocalypse, and why competitions is good, actually
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.

item.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

item.title: Cluely CEO Roy Lee admits to publicly lying about revenue numbers last year
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The $7 million in annual recurring revenue that Cluely CEO Roy Lee shared last summer was a lie, its founder and CEO Roy Lee admitted on Thursday on X.

item.title: Hardware testing startup Nominal hits $1B valuation, raises $155M in 10 months
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Nominal provides hardware testing equipment to defense tech companies. Founders Fund led the preemptive deal.

item.title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

item.title: Zeno raises $25M to speed up production of its battery-swap motorbikes
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: The startup, co-founded by Tesla and Apple alumni, has sold nearly 1,000 of its motorbikes so far.

item.title: Science Corp. raises $230M as it races to bring its brain implant to market
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: A source close to the company says the round assigns Science a post-money valuation of $1.25 billion.

item.title: How 1,000+ customer calls shaped a breakout enterprise AI startup
item.dc:creator: Maggie Nye, Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: On this episode of Build Mode, David Park joins Isabelle Johannessen to discuss how he and his team are intentionally iterating, fundraising, and scaling Narada.

item.title: Lio raises $30M from Andreessen Horowitz and others to automate enterprise procurement
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI procurement startup Lio announced a $30 million Series A in a round led by Andreessen Horowitz.

item.title: His house burned down. He used the insurance money to build PopSockets.
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Does a consumer hardware company need to get on the VC treadmill to succeed? Eleven years and 290 million products sold across 115 countries later, PopSockets has proven that the bootstrapped, low-dilution path more viable than the industry gives it credit for. The global consumer hardware brand was built on less than $500k, no institutional capital, and a philosophy professor’s determination.  Watch as founder and former CEO […]

item.title: How PopSockets broke the VC-backed consumer hardware mold
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Does a consumer hardware company need to get on the VC treadmill to succeed? Eleven years and 290 million products sold across 115 countries later, PopSockets has proven that the bootstrapped, low-dilution path more viable than the industry gives it credit for. The global consumer hardware brand was built on less than $500k, no institutional capital, and a philosophy professor’s determination.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Dominic-Madori Davis […]
